mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% as of April 2018 near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following UNRATE declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 3% year-over-year, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced earnings growth expectations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when activated.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below 0.2% for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 497559 million dollars in April 2018
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with moderating consumer activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% while inflation (CPI) remains below 2.5%, indicating real rate tightening that increases discount rates on future equity cash flows. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both conditions to activate and predicts negative returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.69% and CPIAUCSL at 250.013 in April 2018
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with contained inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met, or 20-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates to below 2% year-over-year, signaling weakening manufacturing activity and reduced corporate capital expenditure, which compresses equity earnings growth expectations over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth falls below 0.15% for one month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with moderating supply-side growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO deceleration are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline by more than 10% quarter-over-quarter, signaling deteriorating residential investment and reduced forward economic growth. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when activated.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1319.0 thousand units in March 2018
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing market weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 jobs per month while unemployment remains below 4%, signaling labor market tightness without sufficient job creation to sustain wage growth and corporate profitability. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148424 thousand and UNRATE at 3.9% in April 2018
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness and slowing job creation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met, or 20-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate, signaling weakening economic momentum and reduced earnings growth expectations. This always-on trend signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17385.831 billion chained 2017 dollars in Q1 2018
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-growth periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate rises above 1.75% while real retail sales growth (RSAFS adjusted for CPIAUCSL) falls below 1.5%, indicating monetary tightening coinciding with weakening real demand. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative returns over 5 trading days when both conditions activate.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1.75% AND real RSAFS growth < 1.5% year-over-year
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with weakening real demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met, or 5-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in triggered periods.
